title: Method of generating sterile terminal sires in livestock and animals produced thereby
abstract: Disclosed herein are animals that are modified genetically to express one or more introduced sry alleles or to have a knockout of an existing sry allele. The disclosure provides an animal comprising a genomic modification of an HMG box of the sry gene. In some embodiments, the modification results in a break in protein synthesis. In other exemplary embodiments, the modification results in an sry protein that fails to bind to its target DNA site or to initiate target synthesis. In yet other exemplary embodiments, the disclosure teaches methods to provide an animal that is genotypically XY and phenotypically male but carries at least one introduced sry allele in a chromosome other than the Y chromosome. In another exemplary embodiment, the disclosure teaches methods to provide a livestock animal that is genotypically XY but is phenotypically female.
claims: Claims 1. A non-human animal having expressed in its genome an exogenous nucleic acid comprising one or more sry alleles. 2. The non-human animal of claim 1, wherein the animal has an X and a Y chromosome and is phenotypically male. 3. The non-human animal of claims 1 or 2, wherein the animal has two X chromosomes and is phenotypically male. 4. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 3, wherein the animal is sterile. 5. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 4, wherein at least one exogenous sry allele is on one or more X chromosomes. 6. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 5, wherein at least one exogenous sry allele is on one or more autosomes. 7. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 6, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced by nonmeiotic introgression. 8. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 7, wherein the exogenous sry alleles are hereditable. 9. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 8, wherein the animal is a livestock animal. 10. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 9, wherein the offspring have two X chromosomes and are phenotypically male. 11. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 10, wherein the offspring are sterile. 12. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 11, wherein the exogenous allele is an ortholog. 13. The non-human animal of claim 1 through 11, wherein the exogenous allele is a paralog. 14. A non-human animal comprising a genomic modification of an HMG box of an sry allele. 15. The non-human animal of claim 14, wherein the animal has an X and a Y chromosome and is phenotypically female. 16. The non-human animal of claims 14 and 15, wherein the animal is sterile. 17. The non-human animal of claims 14 through 16, wherein the animal is a livestock animal. 18. The non-human animal of claims 14 through 17, wherein the modification results in a break in protein synthesis of the gene. 19. The non-human animal of claims 14 through 18, wherein the modification is an insertion or deletion. 20. The non-human animal of claims 14 through 19, wherein the modification results in an inability of sry protein to bind to its target DNA. 21. The non-human animal of claims 1-20, wherein the one or more sry alleles are introduced by transposon transposition. 22. The non-human animal of claims 1-21, wherein the transposon system includes Sleeping Beauty, Passport, Frog Prince, Tol2, or PiggyBac. 23. The non-human animal of claims 1-22, wherein the one or more sry alleles is introduced into the genome without the use of selectable markers. 24. The non-human animal of claims 1-23, wherein the non-human animal is coselected for transposon transposition and non-meiotic introgression. 25. The non-human animal of claims 1-24, wherein non-meiotic introgression interrupts the native sry allele. 26. The non-human animal of claims 1-25, wherein transposon transposition provides multiple insertions of the sry allele. 27. The non-human animal of claims 1-26, wherein the animal is a cow, pig, chicken, goat, sheep, dog, cat, rodent, deer. 28. A method for providing a sterile, phenotypically male non-human animal comprising: introducing one or more sry alleles into the genome of an animal having an XX genotype 29. The method of claim 29, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into an X chromosome. 30. The method of claims 28 and 29, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into both X chromosomes. 31. The method of claims 28 through 30, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into one or more autosomes. 32. The method of claims 28 through 31 , wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into the nucleus of a somatic cell. 33. The method of claims 28 through 32, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are integrated into the genome by nonmeiotic introgression. 34. The method of claims 28 through 33, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into the genome using CRISPR/CAS, zinc finger nuclease, meganuclease, or TALENs technology. 35. The method of claims 28 through 34, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into the genome using transposon systems, recombinant viral techniques, electroporation and microinjection of zygote pronuclei. 36. The method of claims 28 through 35, wherein the somatic cell nucleus is implanted in an enucleated oocyte to provide a renucleated oocyte. 37. The method of claims 28 through 36, wherein the renucleated oocyte is implanted in a surrogate mother. 38. The method of claims 28 through 37, wherein the sry allele is under the control of its native promoter. 39. The method of claims 28 through 38, wherein the sry allele is under the control of a non-native promoter. 40. The method of claims 28 through 39, wherein the promoter is an inducible promoter or a constitutive promoter. 41. The method of claims 28 through 40, wherein the inducible promoter comprises tetracycline, doxycycline, ecdysone, rapamycin, Hsp70.3, LAC or TRE. 42. The method of claims 28 through 41, wherein the constitutive promoter comprises CMV, CaMV 35s, SV40, CMV, UBC, EF1A, PGK and CAGG. 43. The method of claims 28 through 42, wherein the sry allele is injected into the pronucleus of a zygote. 44. The method of claims 28 through 43, wherein the sry allele is injected into the cytoplasm of a zygote. 45. The method of claims 28 through 44, wherein the allele is injected as RNA directly into a zygote using TALEN or ZFN RNA, CRISPR/CAS or meganuclease technology. 46. The method of claims 28 through 45, wherein the non-human animal is a livestock animal. 47. The method of claims 21 through 46, wherein the sry allele is an ortholog. 48. The method of claim s 28 through 47, wherein the sry allele is a paralog. 49. A method for making a sterile, phenotypically female livestock having an XY genotype comprising; a genomic modification an HMG box of an sry allele. 50. The method of claims 49, wherein the modification results in a break in protein synthesis of a gene. 51. The method of claims 49 and 50, wherein the modification results in improper binding of the sry protein to its target gene. 52. The method of claims 49 through 51 , wherein the modification comprises and insert or a deletion. 53. The method of claims 49 through 52, wherein the modification is made in a somatic cell or the nucleus of a zygote. 54. The method of claims 49 through 53, wherein the somatic cell nucleus is transferred into an enucleated egg to provide a renuclated egg. 55. The method of claims 49 through 54, wherein the renucleated egg is implanted into the uterus of a surrogate mother. 56. The method of claims 49 through 55, wherein the genomic modification results from direct injection of specific nucleotides into a zygote. 57. The method of claims 49 through 56, wherein the modification is carried out using precision gene editing using zinc finger nuclease, meganuclease, TALENs or CRISPR/CAS technology. 58. The method of claims 49 through 57, wherein the modification is an insertion, deletion or single nucleotide polymorphism resulting from non-homologous end joining. 59. The non-human animal of claims 1 through 58, wherein the animal comprises an animal genetically modified to express one or more introduced sry alleles without the use of a selectable marker. 60. An animal cell having in its genome an introduced nucleic acid comprising one or more sry alleles. 61. The animal cell of claim 60, wherein the animal has an X and a Y chromosome and is phenotypically male. 62. The animal cell of claims 60 or 61, wherein the animal has two X chromosomes and is phenotypically male. 63. The animal cell of claims 60 through 62, wherein the animal is sterile. 64. The animal cell of claims 60 through 63, wherein at least one exogenous sry allele is on one or more X chromosomes. 65. The animal cell of claims 60 through 64, wherein at least one exogenous sry allele is on one or more autosomes. 66. The animal cell of claims 60 through 65, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced by nonmeiotic introgression. 67. The animal cell of claims 60 through 66, wherein the one or more exogenous sry alleles is introduced into the genome using a transposon system. 68. The animal cell of claims 60 through 67, wherein the transposon system is Sleeping Beauty, Passport, Frog Prince, Tol2, or PiggyBac. 69. The animal cell of claims 60 through 68 where the one or more exogenous sry alleles is introduced into the genome without the use of selectable markers. 70. The animal cell of claims 60 through 69 wherein the non-human animal is coselected for transposon transposition and non-meiotic introgression. 71. The animal cell of claims 60 through 70, wherein non-meiotic introgression interrupts the native sry allele. 72. The animal cell of claims 60 through 71, wherein the exogenous sry alleles are hereditable. 73. The animal cell of claims 60 through 72, wherein the animal is a livestock animal cell. 74. The animal cell of claims 60 through 73, wherein the cell is a primary cell, primary somatic cell or zygote. 75. The animal cell of claims 60 through 74, wherein transposon transposition provides multiple insertions of the sry allele. 76. The animal cell of claims 60 through 75, wherein the cell is a non-human cell. 77. The animal cell of any of claims 60 through 76, wherein the cell is cotransformed by transposon transposition and non-meiotic introgression. 78. The animal cell of claims 60 through 77, wherein the cell is a cow, pig, chicken, goat, sheep, dog, cat, rodent, deer, vole or rabbit cell. 79. The animal cell of claims 60 through 78, wherein the exogenous allele is an ortholog. 80. The animal cell of claims 60 through 79, wherein the exogenous allele is a paralog. 81. An animal cell comprising a genomic modification of a native sry allele. 82 The animal cell of claims 60 through 81, wherein the genomic modification is a modification of the HMG box. 83. The animal cell of claims 60 through 82, wherein the modification is made by non- meiotic introgression. 84. The animal cell of claims 60 through 83, wherein the cell has an X and a Y chromosome. 85. The animal cell of claims 60 through 84, wherein the cell is a primary cell, a primary somatic cell or a zygote. 86. The animal cell of claims 60 through 85, wherein the cell is a non-human animal cell. 87. The animal cell of claims 60 through 86, wherein the modification is an insertion or deletion. 88. A non-human animal made from any of the cells or methods of any of the preceding claims. 89. A process for making a non-human animal cell according to any of the preceding claims wherein one or more exogenous sry alleles are introduced into the cells genome. 90. The process of claim 89, wherein the introduction is accomplished using zinc finger nuclease, meganuclease, TALENs or CRISPR/CAS technology. 91. The process of claim 89, wherein the introduction is accomplished using transposon technology. 92. The process of claims 89 through 91, wherein the cell is a primary cell, primary somatic cell or zygote. 93. A cell derived from the process of any of claims 89-92. 94. A non-human animal derived from the cell of claim 93.
